Details: Degradation of disulfoton by direct photolysis using polychormatic irradiation is a very slow process. It takes 130 hours for 83 percent of disulfoton (100 μg/L) to be degraded by polychromatic irradiation ( λ > 285 nm) in lab water [1808]. This irradiation time increases when the experiment was carried out in surface water. These long irradiation times are not typical for a drinking water treatment.
